differentials.name: Allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis with central bronchiectasis
differentials.raw: Allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis with central bronchiectasis

context: =  chronic episodic reversible bronchoconstriction (= airflow obstruction) ← hypersensitivity / hyperresponsiveness of airways to a variety of stimuli Terminology: Reactive airway disease = clinical diagnosis of asthma not yet established, usually in children between 2 and 6 years of age Age:   middle age Pathogenesis: probably autoimmune phenomenon caused by viral respiratory infection and often provoked by infection, exercise, pharmaceuticals; NO environmental antigen Pathogenesis: antigens produce an immediate hypersensitivity response (type I); reagin sensitizes mast cells → histamine rele
